tooltips.targetVolume: The loudness every tab converges toward. Higher = louder overall. -14 LUFS matches most streaming platforms.
tooltips.lufs: LUFS = Loudness Units Full Scale. A perceptual loudness standard (EBU R128) that approximates how loud content sounds to human ears, unlike raw dB peaks.

tooltips.title: A safety valve at the end of each tab's audio chain. Prevents harsh clipping distortion when boosted audio would exceed 0 dBFS.
tooltips.threshold: The level above which the limiter kicks in. Everything quieter passes through untouched.
tooltips.ratio: How hard the limiter pushes back once the threshold is crossed. 20:1 means 20 dB over becomes 1 dB out — the maximum, effectively a brick wall.
tooltips.attack: How fast the limiter reacts to spikes. Fast = catches transients (drums, pops); slow = more natural but lets some peaks through.
tooltips.release: How fast the limiter lets go after the peak passes. Fast = punchy but may pump; slow = smoother but recovers later.
tooltips.knee: The transition sharpness around the threshold. Hard = sudden compression (precise); soft = gradual (less audible).

empty.title: Waiting for audio...
empty.hint: Open a tab with audio and it will be balanced automatically

balance.onHint: Volume balance: ON (click to bypass / A-B)
balance.offHint: Volume balance: OFF — passthrough (click to re-enable)

updateNotice.text: EqualLoud was updated. Refresh your playing tabs to restore balance.
